ddls:
CREATE TABLE "pavement_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "age_month" INTEGER /* Age of the pavement in months */,
  "age_year" REAL /* Age of the pavement in years */,
  "rupture_modulus" REAL /* Modulus of rupture (psi) */,
  "elasticity_modulus" REAL /* Modulus of elasticity (ksi) */,
  "stress_concrete_T" REAL /* Tensile stress in concrete (psi) */,
  "stress_concrete_E" REAL /* Elastic stress in concrete (psi) */,
  "total_concrete_stress" REAL /* Total stress in concrete (psi) */,
  "stress_strength_ratio" REAL /* Stress to strength ratio (psi/psi) */,
  "repetitions_to_failure" INTEGER /* Repetitions to failure */,
  "pavement_damage" REAL /* Pavement damage metric */,
  "cumulative_damage" REAL /* Cumulative pavement damage */,
  "punchouts_per_mile" REAL /* Number of punchouts per mile */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site associated with the metric */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the metrics were collected */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Ambient temperature during the measurement */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Ambient humidity during the measurement */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions during the measurement */,
  "traffic_volume" INTEGER /* Volume of traffic at the time of measurement */,
  "maintenance_history_id" INTEGER /* ID of the maintenance history entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pavement_metrics_maintenance_history_id FOREIGN KEY ("maintenance_history_id") REFERENCES maintenance_history ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pavement_metrics_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the pavement site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the pavement site */,
  "construction_date" TEXT /* Date of construction of the pavement */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the site */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the site */,
  "pavement_type" TEXT /* Type of pavement (e.g., concrete, asphalt) */,
  "pavement_thickness" REAL /* Thickness of the pavement */,
  "pavement_width" REAL /* Width of the pavement */,
  "design_load" REAL /* Design load for the pavement */,
  "last_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the last inspection */,
  "next_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled inspection */,
  "maintenance_history_id" INTEGER /* ID of the maintenance history entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site associated with the report */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric entry used in the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "findings" TEXT /* Findings from the analysis of the metrics */,
  "recommendations" TEXT /* Recommendations based on the findings */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the engineer who created the report */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the engineer who approved the report */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date the report was approved */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, submitted, approved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES pavement_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., engineering, maintenance, administration) */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric entry triggering the alert */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site associated with the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the nature of the alert */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the engineer who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES pavement_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_history" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance entry */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site where maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., repair, inspection, replacement) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the maintenance work */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  "performed_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the engineer who performed the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_history_performed_by FOREIGN KEY ("performed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_history_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id")
)
